role_title: Operations Program Manager Computer Vision, Public Sector

location.office_time_required: regularly work onsite in both unclassified and secure facilities

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in a general manager / program manager position or a similar leadership role in operations
Collaborating with a team of high performing operations managers and subject matter experts
Base salary range up to $212,000 in DC/Hawaii

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in a general manager / program manager position or a similar leadership role in operations
The ability to regularly work onsite in both unclassified and secure facilities in either downtown Saint Louis, MO or the Washington D.C. area
An analytical, automation-focused mindset, able to analyze provable and traceable data to develop robust, flexible integrations and workflows with metrics-led results
Experience working in a fast-paced environment
Low ego and a proven pattern of sharing wins with your team
A history of successful project management and comfort in ambiguity
Willingness to travel up to 25% of the time based on customer + mission needs

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A technical background (education or professional experience with economics, statistics, computer science, or engineering)
Experience working in and / or with the U.S. government
A deep understanding of ML operations for Computer Vision and Generative AI workflows / products

scope.surface: Public Sector Computer Vision team — data annotation and labeling operations for DoD and national security customers
scope.business_model: B2G (government contracts) — data labeling and AI/ML enablement for DoD and national security
primary_metrics_inferred: ML outcome optimization for government customers
core_responsibilities: Design and lead cross-functional initiatives with engineering, product, analytics, and delivery teams (as well as relevant geospatial experts) to achieve project outcomes
Innovate on complex computer vision workflows and evaluation of data quality
Communicate and own the execution of multiple data annotation workflows across diverse data types (geospatial imagery, video, etc)
Optimize data annotation workflows with systems analysis to improve data quality and throughput with a goal achieving optimized machine learning outcomes
Outline and deliver on data-driven operational efficiencies on these workflows to achieve business outcomes
Partner with an engagement managers to communicate with and shape outcomes for Scale's Public Sector customers

compensation.equity_form: equity grant (subject to Board of Director approval)

green_flags: Compensation ranges explicitly disclosed for two locations (DC/Hawaii: $139,200-$212,000; St. Louis/Suffolk: $116,000-$177,000)
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Clear product surface and ownership area (Public Sector CV team)
TS-SCI clearance is nice-to-have, not hard required — lowers barrier to entry
Comprehensive benefits including health, dental, vision, retirement, L&D stipend, and generous PTO mentioned
red_flags: Role requires regular onsite presence in classified/secure facilities — eliminates remote candidates entirely
Work authorization and visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for non-citizens given national security context
DoD/national security context likely implies de facto US citizenship requirement even if not stated
90-day waiting period policy noted for reconsideration of same-role candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years + compensation + team ownership signals)
